songs.title: I Forgot That You Existed
songs.lyrics:
How many days did I spend thinking
'Bout how you did me wrong, wrong, wrong?
Lived in the shade you were throwing
'Til all of my sunshine was gone, gone, gone
And I couldn't get away from ya
In my feelings more than Drake, so yeah
Your name on my lips, tongue-tied
Free rent, living in my mind

But then something happened one magical night

I forgot that you existed
And I thought that it would kill me, but it didn't
And it was so nice
So peaceful and quiet
I forgot that you existed
It isn't love, it isn't hate, it's just indifference
I forgot that you

Got out some popcorn
As soon as my rep started going down, down, down
Laughed on the school yard
As soon as I tripped up and hit the ground, ground, ground
And I would've stuck around for ya
Would've fought the whole town, so yeah
Would've been right there, front row
Even if nobody came to your show

But you showed who you are, then one magical night

I forgot that you existed
And I thought that it would kill me, but it didn't
And it was so nice
So peaceful and quiet
I forgot that you existed
It isn't love, it isn't hate, it's just indifference
I forgot that you

Sent me a clear message
Taught me some hard lessons
I just forget what they were
It's all just a blur

I forgot that you existed
And I thought that it would kill me, but it didn't
And it was so nice
So peaceful and quiet
I forgot that you existed
I did, I did, I did
It isn't hate, it's just indifference
It isn't love, it isn't hate, it's just indifference
So, yeah

vocab.definition: A lack of interest or concern. It is a state of not being affected or involved in something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: it isn't love, it isn't hate, it's just indifference
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her emotional state after forgetting about the person, implying a sense of neutrality and detachment. She's no longer invested in the relationship or the person's actions.

vocab.definition: Extremely pleasant or exciting. It can also refer to something that seems to have a special or mysterious quality.
vocab.lyric_snippet: something happened one magical night
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the night when she finally forgot about the person, implying that it was a transformative and special moment.

vocab.definition: Unable to speak, often due to being nervous or embarrassed. It can also refer to being unable to express oneself fluently or clearly.
vocab.lyric_snippet: your name on my lips, tongue-tied
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her inability to speak or express herself when thinking about the person, implying a sense of emotional overwhelm or anxiety.

vocab.definition: A person's reputation or character, especially in relation to their social standing or professional image.
vocab.lyric_snippet: as soon as my rep started going down
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how the person reacted when her reputation or social standing began to decline, implying a sense of schadenfreude or taking pleasure in someone else's misfortune.

vocab.definition: Something that is difficult to see or remember clearly, often due to being unclear or indistinct. It can also refer to a state of confusion or disorientation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: it's all just a blur
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her memories of the person and the lessons they taught her, implying a sense of confusion or uncertainty.

songs.title: Cruel Summer
songs.lyrics:
(Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah)

Fever dream high in the quiet of the night
You know that I caught it (Oh yeah, you're right, I want it)
Bad, bad boy, shiny toy with a price
You know that I bought it (Oh yeah, you're right, I want it)

Killing me slow, out the window
I'm always waiting for you to be waiting below
Devils roll the dice, angels roll their eyes
What doesn't kill me makes me want you more

And it's new, the shape of your body
It's blue, the feeling I've got
And it's ooh, woah-oh
It's a cruel summer
It's cool, that's what I tell 'em
No rules in breakable heaven
But ooh, woah-oh
It's a cruel summer with you

Hang your head low in the glow of the vending machine
I'm not dying (
Oh yeah, you're right, I want it
)
We say that we'll just screw it up in these trying times
We're not trying (Oh yeah, you're right, I want it)

So cut the headlights, summer's a knife
I'm always waiting for you just to cut to the bone
Devils roll the dice, angels roll their eyes
And if I bleed, you'll be the last to know, oh

It's new, the shape of your body
It's blue, the feeling I've got
And it's ooh, woah-oh
It's a cruel summer
It's cool, that's what I tell 'em
No rules in breakable heaven
But ooh, woah-oh
It's a cruel summer with you

I'm drunk in the back of the car
And I cried like a baby comin' home from the bar (Oh)
Said, "I'm fine," but it wasn't true
I don't wanna keep secrets just to keep you
And I snuck in through the garden gate
Every night that summer just to seal my fate (Oh)
And I scream, "For whatever it's worth
I love you, ain't that the worst thing you ever heard?"
He looks up, grinnin' like a devil

It's new, the shape of your body
It's blue, the feeling I've got
And it's ooh, woah-oh
It's a cruel summer
It's cool, that's what I tell 'em
No rules in breakable heaven
But ooh, woah-oh
It's a cruel summer with you

I'm drunk in the back of the car
And I cried like a baby comin' home from the bar (Oh)
Said, "I'm fine," but it wasn't true
I don't wanna keep secrets just to keep you
And I snuck in through the garden gate
Every night that summer just to seal my fate (Oh)
And I scream, "For whatever it's worth
I love you, ain't that the worst thing you ever heard?"
(Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah)

vocab.definition: A fever is a state of abnormally high body temperature, often caused by illness or infection. In a broader sense, it can also refer to an intense enthusiasm or excitement.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Fever dream high in the quiet
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'fever' to describe the intense, all-consuming nature of her desire, drawing a parallel between physical and emotional fervor.

vocab.definition: Capable of being broken or damaged, often referring to physical objects, but can also describe something fragile or vulnerable.
vocab.lyric_snippet: No rules in breakable heaven
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'breakable' to describe a 'heaven' that, despite its idealized nature, is fragile and susceptible to damage, highlighting the risks and vulnerabilities in her relationship.

vocab.definition: A vending machine is a device that dispenses goods, such as snacks or drinks, when money is inserted. The term 'vending' can also refer to the act of selling or dispensing something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Hang your head low in the glow of the vending machine
vocab.context: Taylor uses the image of a vending machine to create a vivid, late-night scene, possibly symbolizing a moment of solitude or a search for comfort.

vocab.definition: To seal something means to close or fasten it securely, often to prevent escape or leakage. It can also mean to confirm or ratify something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: just to seal my fate
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'seal' to imply that her actions are determining her destiny, suggesting a sense of inevitability or commitment to the path she's chosen.

vocab.definition: Grinning means smiling widely, often in a mischievous or self-satisfied way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: He looks up, grinnin' like a devil
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'grinnin'' to describe the other person's reaction, implying a sense of sly amusement or pleasure, which contrasts with her own emotional turmoil.

songs.title: Lover
songs.lyrics:
We could leave the Christmas lights up 'til January
And this is our place, we make the rules
And there's a dazzling haze, a mysterious way about you, dear
Have I known you twenty seconds or twenty years?

Can I go where you go?
Can we always be this close?
Forever and ever, ah
Take me out and take me home
You're my, my, my, my lover

We could let our friends crash in the living room
This is our place, we make the call
And I'm highly suspicious that everyone who sees you wants you
I've loved you three summers now, honey, but I want 'em all

Can I go where you go?
Can we always be this close?
Forever and ever, ah
Take me out and take me home (Forever and ever)
You're my, my, my, my lover

Ladies and gentlemen, will you please stand?
With every guitar string scar on my hand
I take this magnetic force of a man to be my lover
My heart's been borrowed and yours has been blue
All's well that ends well to end up with you
Swear to be overdramatic and true to my lover
And you'll save all your dirtiest jokes for me
And at every table, I'll save you a seat, lover

Can I go where you go?
Can we always be this close?
Forever and ever, ah
Take me out and take me home (Forever and ever)
You're my, my, my, my
Oh, you're my, my, my, my
Darling, you're my, my, my, my lover

vocab.definition: extremely impressive or attractive, often because of being bright or colorful
vocab.lyric_snippet: a dazzling haze, a mysterious way
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'dazzling' to describe the captivating aura surrounding her lover, emphasizing the strong attraction she feels.

vocab.definition: difficult to understand or explain, often because of being secret or obscure
vocab.lyric_snippet: a mysterious way about you, dear
vocab.context: The 'mysterious' quality of her lover adds to the allure and enchantment Taylor feels in the relationship.

vocab.definition: having or showing a feeling that someone is doing something wrong or has a hidden motive
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm highly suspicious that everyone who sees you wants you
vocab.context: Taylor's 'suspicious' nature hints at her awareness of her lover's attractiveness to others and her own possessiveness.

vocab.definition: having a strong and attractive quality that draws people to you
vocab.lyric_snippet: this magnetic force of a man
vocab.context: Taylor describes her lover as a 'magnetic force', emphasizing his charismatic and attractive personality.

vocab.definition: exaggerated or excessively emotional in behavior or reaction
vocab.lyric_snippet: Swear to be overdramatic and true to my lover
vocab.context: Taylor promises to be 'overdramatic' in her expressions of love, showcasing her willingness to be emotionally intense and genuine.

songs.title: The Man
songs.lyrics:
I would be complex, I would be cool
They'd say I played the field before I found someone to commit to
And that would be okay for me to do
Every conquest I had made would make me more of a boss to you

I’d be a fearless leader, I'd be an alpha type
When everyone believes ya, what's that like?

I’m so sick of running as fast as I can
Wonderin' if I'd get there quicker if I was a man
And I'm so sick of them comin' at me again
'Cause if I was a man, then I'd be the man
I'd be the man (Man)
I’d be the man (Man)

They’d say I hustled, put in the work
They wouldn't shake their heads and question how much of this I deserve
What I was wearing, if I was rude
Could all be separated from my good ideas and power moves

And they would toast to me, oh (Ayy), let the players play
I’d be just like Leo in Saint-Tropez

I'm so sick of running as fast as I can
Wonderin' if I'd get there quicker if I was a man
And I’m so sick of them comin' at me again
'Cause if I was a man, then I'd be the man
I'd be the man (Man)
I'd be the man (Man)

What's it like to brag about raking in dollars
And getting bitches and models?
And it's all good if you're bad
And it's okay if you're mad
If I was out flashin' my dollars
I'd be a bitch, not a baller
They'd paint me out to be bad
So it's okay that I'm mad

I'm so sick of running as fast as I can
Wonderin' if I'd get there quicker if I was a man (You know that)
And I'm so sick of them comin' at me again (Comin' at me again)
'Cause if I was a man (If I was a man), then I'd be the man (Then I'd be the man)
I'm so sick of running as fast as I can (As fast as I can)
Wonderin' if I'd get there quicker if I was a man (Hey)
And I'm so sick of them comin' at me again (Comin' at me again)
'Cause if I was a man (If I was a man), then I'd be the man
I'd be the man (Man)
I'd be the man (Man, oh)
I'd be the man (Man, yeah)
I'd be the man (Man, I'd be the man)

If I was a man
Then I'd be the man

vocab.definition: The act of successfully taking control of a place or situation, often through force or effort. In a broader sense, it can also refer to achieving a personal goal or overcoming a challenge.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Every conquest I had made
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'conquest' to describe the accomplishments and successes she would have if she were a man, implying that these achievements would be viewed more positively and contribute to her status as a 'boss'.

vocab.definition: An individual, typically male, who is considered to be the most dominant or powerful within a social group. The term is often used to describe someone who exhibits confidence, leadership, and a strong sense of self.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'd be an alpha type
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'alpha' to describe the type of leader she would be if she were a man, suggesting that she would be confident, dominant, and respected.

vocab.definition: To work very hard, often in a competitive or challenging environment, in order to achieve success or make progress. It can also imply a sense of urgency or aggressiveness in one's efforts.
vocab.lyric_snippet: They'd say I hustled, put in the work
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'hustled' to describe the effort and dedication she would be recognized for if she were a man, implying that her hard work would be valued and respected.

vocab.definition: To publicly express praise or appreciation for someone, often with a drink. It can also refer to a speech or statement made in someone's honor.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And they would toast to me
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'toast' to describe the admiration and respect she would receive if she were a man, implying that her accomplishments would be celebrated and recognized.

vocab.definition: A slang term used to describe someone who is very successful, often in a flashy or impressive way. It can imply a sense of wealth, status, or prestige.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'd be a baller
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'baller' to describe the kind of respect and admiration she would receive if she were a man, suggesting that she would be seen as successful and impressive.

vocab.definition: To gather or collect something, often in large quantities. In this context, it refers to earning or making a lot of money.
vocab.lyric_snippet: raking in dollars
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'raking' to describe the ease with which men can earn and spend money, implying that they are often praised for their financial success.

songs.title: The Archer
songs.lyrics:
Combat, I'm ready for combat
I say I don't want that, but what if I do?
'Cause cruelty wins in the movies
I've got a hundred thrown-out speeches I almost said to you

Easy they come, easy they go
I jump from the train, I ride off alone
I never grew up, it's getting so old
Help me hold on to you

I've been the archer, I've been the prey
Who could ever leave me, darling
But who could stay?

Dark side, I search for your dark side
But what if I'm alright, right, right, right here?
And I cut off my nose just to spite my face
Then I hate my reflection for years and years

I wake in the night, I pace like a ghost
The room is on fire, invisible smoke
And all of my heroes die all alone
Help me hold on to you

I've been the archer, I've been the prey
Screaming, who could ever leave me, darling
But who could stay?
(I see right through me, I see right through me)

'Cause they see right through me
They see right through me
They see right through
Can you see right through me?
They see right through
They see right through me
I see right through me
I see right through me

All the king's horses, all the king's men
Couldn't put me together again
'Cause all of my enemies started out friends
Help me hold on to you

I've been the archer, I've been the prey
Who could ever leave me, darling
But who could stay?
(I see right through me, I see right through me)
Who could stay?
Who could stay?
Who could stay?
You could stay
You could stay

Combat, I'm ready for combat

vocab.definition: The quality of being cruel, which means having or showing a desire to hurt or inflict pain on others. It can also refer to a cruel act or situation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: cruelty wins in the movies
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'cruelty' to describe how it often prevails in dramatic or intense situations, like in movies, and perhaps in her own life as well.

vocab.definition: An animal that is hunted or killed by another animal for food. It can also refer to someone who is vulnerable to attack or exploitation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I've been the archer, I've been the prey
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'prey' to describe herself as someone vulnerable, contrasting with her role as 'the archer', who is in control.

vocab.definition: A feeling of ill will or resentment, often leading to a desire to hurt or annoy someone. It can also refer to an action taken to hurt or annoy someone.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I cut off my nose just to spite my face
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'spite' to describe self-destructive behavior, where she hurts herself in an attempt to prove a point or get back at someone.

vocab.definition: Unable to be seen or noticed. It can also refer to something that is not apparent or obvious.
vocab.lyric_snippet: invisible smoke
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'invisible' to describe the emotional turmoil she's experiencing, which is not visible to others but still very real to her.

vocab.definition: People who are admired or idealized for their courage, achievements, or noble qualities.
vocab.lyric_snippet: and all of my heroes die all alone
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'heroes' to describe people she looks up to, but notes that even they can be flawed and suffer alone.

songs.title: I Think He Knows
songs.lyrics:
I think he knows
His footprints on the sidewalk
Lead to where I can't stop
Go there every night
I think he knows
His hands around a cold glass
Make me wanna know that
Body like it's mine

He got that boyish look that I like in a man
I am an architect, I'm drawing up the plans
It's like I'm 17, nobody understands
No one understands

He got my heartbeat
Skipping down 16th Avenue
Got that, ah, I mean
Wanna see what's under that attitude like
I want you, bless my soul
And I ain't gotta tell him, I think he knows
I think he knows

I think he knows
When we get all alone
I'll make myself at home
And he'll want me to stay
I think he knows
He'd better lock it down
Or I won't stick around

Boy, I understand

Got that, ah, I mean (I mean)
Wanna see what's under that attitude like
I want you, bless my soul
And I ain't gotta tell him, I think he knows
I think he knows

I want you, bless my
I want you, bless my
I want you, bless my soul

Lyrical smile, indigo eyes, hand on my thigh

Lyrical smile, indigo eyes, hand on my thigh

"So where we gonna go?"
I whisper in the dark
"Where we gonna go?"
I think he knows

He got my heartbeat (Heartbeat)
Skipping down 16th Avenue (Baby)
Got that, ah, I mean (I mean)
Wanna see what's under that attitude like (Yeah)
I want you, bless my soul
And I ain't gotta tell him, I think he knows

I want you, bless my

I want you, bless my soul (He got my heartbeat)
I want you, bless my (Skipping down 16th Avenue, baby)
I want you, bless my
I want you, bless my soul
And I ain't gotta tell him, I think he knows

vocab.definition: A person who designs buildings and other structures. In a broader sense, it can also refer to someone who plans or devises something, like a plan or a project.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I am an architect, I'm drawing up the plans
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe herself as a planner, drawing up plans for a relationship, showing her proactive and intentional approach to romance.

vocab.definition: Having a youthful or immature quality, often in a charming or attractive way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: He got that boyish look that I like in a man
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a charming, youthful quality in someone she's interested in, highlighting her attraction to this trait.

vocab.definition: A deep blue color, often used to describe something that is a deep, rich blue.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Lyrical smile, indigo eyes, hand on my thigh
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the color of someone's eyes, adding a poetic and vivid detail to her description of this person.

vocab.definition: Having a musical quality, often used to describe something that is poetic or expressive.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Lyrical smile, indigo eyes, hand on my thigh
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone's smile, suggesting that it has a musical or poetic quality that she finds attractive.

vocab.definition: Completely fascinated or fixated on something or someone, often to an excessive degree.
vocab.lyric_snippet: He's so obsessed with me and, boy, I understand
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone's intense interest in her, suggesting that they are completely captivated by her.

songs.title: Miss Americana & the Heartbreak Prince
songs.lyrics:
You know I adore you, I'm crazier for you
Than I was at sixteen, lost in a film scene
Waving homecoming queens, marching band playing
I'm lost in the lights
American glory faded before me
Now I'm feeling hopeless, ripped up my prom dress
Running through rose thorns, I saw the scoreboard
And ran for my life (Ah)

No cameras catch my pageant smile
I counted days, I counted miles
To see you there, to see you there
It's been a long time coming, but

It's you and me, that's my whole world
They whisper in the hallway, "She's a bad, bad girl" (Okay)
The whole school is rolling fake dice
You play stupid games, you win stupid prizes
It's you and me, there's nothing like this
Miss Americana and The Heartbreak Prince (Okay)
We're so sad, we paint the town blue
Voted most likely to run away with you

My team is losing, battered and bruising
I see the high fives between the bad guys
Leave with my head hung, you are the only one
Who seems to care
American stories burning before me
I'm feeling helpless, the damsels are depressed
Boys will be boys then, where are the wise men?
Darling, I'm scared (Ah)

No cameras catch my muffled cries
I counted days, I counted miles
To see you there, to see you there
And now the storm is coming, but

It's you and me, that's my whole world
They whisper in the hallway, "She's a bad, bad girl" (Okay)
The whole school is rolling fake dice
You play stupid games, you win stupid prizes
It's you and me, there's nothing like this
Miss Americana and The Heartbreak Prince (Okay)
We're so sad, we paint the town blue
Voted most likely to run away with you

And I don't want you to (Go), I don't really wanna (Fight)
'Cause nobody's gonna (Win), I think you should come home
And I don't want you to (Go), I don't really wanna (Fight)
'Cause nobody's gonna (Win), I think you should come home
And I don't want you to (Go), I don't really wanna (Fight)
'Cause nobody's gonna (Win), just thought you should know
And I'll never let you (Go) 'cause I know this is a (Fight)
That someday we're gonna (Win)

It's you and me, that's my whole world
They whisper in the hallway, "She's a bad, bad girl"
Oh, I just thought you should know (You should know)
It's you and me, there's nothing like this (Like this)
Miss Americana and The Heartbreak Prince (Okay)
We're so sad, we paint the town blue (Paint it blue)
Voted most likely to run away with you

And I don't want you to (Go), I don't really wanna (Fight)
'Cause nobody's gonna (Win), I think you should come home
And I'll never let you (Go) 'cause I know this is a (Fight)
That someday we're gonna (Win), just thought you should know

It's you and me, that's my whole world
They whisper in the hallway, "She's a bad, bad girl"
"She's a bad, bad girl"

vocab.definition: To love or respect someone or something very much. It is a strong feeling of affection or admiration.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You know I adore you
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'adore' to express her deep affection for someone, setting the tone for the rest of the song.

vocab.definition: A public entertainment, especially one with a procession or parade, often with a competitive element. In this context, it refers to a perfect or fake smile.
vocab.lyric_snippet: No cameras catch my pageant smile
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pageant' to describe a fake or perfect smile, highlighting the contrast between her public image and true feelings.

vocab.definition: A woman or girl, especially one in a difficult or distressing situation, often portrayed as needing to be rescued.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the damsels are depressed
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'damsels' to refer to women who are depressed or in distress, possibly commenting on societal expectations and pressures on women.

vocab.definition: To reduce the loudness or clarity of a sound, often by covering or wrapping something around it. In this context, it refers to suppressed or hidden emotions.
vocab.lyric_snippet: No cameras catch my muffled cries
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'muffled' to describe her suppressed emotions, highlighting the contrast between her public image and true feelings.

vocab.definition: Physically or emotionally damaged, often as a result of repeated or severe punishment, abuse, or hardship.
vocab.lyric_snippet: My team is losing, battered and bruising
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'battered' to describe her team's physical or emotional state, possibly commenting on the challenges and hardships they face.

vocab.definition: Shining or burning unsteadily, with a faint or intermittent light.

vocab.definition: Without law or government; chaotic and unordered.

vocab.definition: A weak or cowardly way to avoid dealing with a situation or taking responsibility for one's actions. In this context, it refers to hiding behind a tweet instead of confronting someone in person.

vocab.definition: The act of expressing one's thoughts, feelings, or personality, often through creative or artistic means. In this context, it refers to the person's right to express themselves, even if Taylor disagrees with their opinions.

vocab.definition: To be excessively preoccupied with something or someone, often to the point of being unhealthy or unproductive. In this context, it refers to wasting time and energy on negative thoughts or emotions.

vocab.context: Taylor reflects on her past experiences and realizes that obsessing over others' opinions or actions is not worth her time or energy.

vocab.definition: The Gay & Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation, an organization that promotes LGBTQ+ rights and acceptance. In this context, it's used to encourage the person to support and celebrate the LGBTQ+ community instead of spreading hate.

vocab.context: Taylor references the organization to promote a message of love, acceptance, and inclusivity.

vocab.definition: A period of time characterized by a lack of cultural, intellectual, or social progress. In this context, it refers to a mindset that is intolerant, close-minded, and resistant to change.

vocab.context: Taylor implies that the person's hateful and intolerant behavior is outdated and unenlightened, belonging to a bygone era.

vocab.definition: Strong and often uncontrollable desires or impulses. In this context, it refers to the person's inclination to lash out or spread hate.

vocab.context: Taylor advises the person to manage their emotions and impulses, rather than giving in to hatred or anger.

vocab.definition: A subtle or indirect insult, often delivered through a clever or sarcastic comment. In this context, it refers to the person's attempts to belittle or mock others.
vocab.lyric_snippet: shade never made anybody less gay
vocab.context: Taylor argues that throwing shade or insults does not achieve anything positive and only serves to perpetuate negativity.

vocab.definition: a person, especially a child, who is difficult to control or manage. Someone who requires a lot of attention or effort to deal with.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I know that I'm a handful, baby
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe herself as someone who can be challenging to be in a relationship with, but in a charming way.

vocab.definition: a person who is considered to be mentally unstable or violent. In this context, it means acting wildly or irrationally.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I know that I went psycho on the phone
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her behavior in a past argument, implying that she lost control of her emotions.

vocab.definition: uninteresting, boring, or of poor quality. In this context, it refers to people who are uncool or unimpressive.
vocab.lyric_snippet: (And there's a lotta lame guys out there)
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to contrast herself with others, implying that she is more exciting and interesting than the average person.

songs.title: It's Nice to Have a Friend
songs.lyrics:
Ooh
Ooh

School bell rings, walk me home
Sidewalk chalk covered in snow
Lost my gloves, you give me one
"Wanna hang out?" Yeah, sounds like fun
Video games, you pass me a note
Sleeping in tents

It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)
It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)

Light pink sky, up on the roof
Sun sinks down, no curfew
Twenty questions, we tell the truth
You've been stressed out lately, yeah, me too
Something gave you the nerve
To touch my hand

It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)
It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)

Church bells ring, carry me home
Rice on the ground looks like snow
Call my bluff, call you "Babe"
Have my back, yeah, every day
Feels like home, stay in bed
The whole weekend

It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)
It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)
It's nice to have a friend (Ooh)
(Ooh)

vocab.definition: A rule or law that requires people to be at home by a certain time, usually at night. It is often imposed by parents or authorities.
vocab.lyric_snippet: no curfew
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'curfew' to describe the freedom and relaxation of spending time with a friend, implying that they can stay out as late as they want without worrying about rules or restrictions.

vocab.definition: The courage or confidence to do something, often something that is difficult or intimidating. It can also refer to a feeling of boldness or audacity.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Something gave you the nerve
vocab.context: In this song, Taylor uses 'nerve' to describe the moment when her friend gained the confidence to make a romantic gesture, such as touching her hand.

vocab.definition: To pretend or deceive someone, often in a playful or lighthearted way. It can also refer to a false or misleading statement.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Call my bluff
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bluff' to describe a playful and teasing interaction with her friend, where they challenge each other and test each other's confidence.

songs.title: Daylight
songs.lyrics:
My love was as cruel as the cities I lived in
Everyone looked worse in the light
There are so many lines that I've crossed unforgiven
I'll tell you the truth but never goodbye

I don't wanna look at anything else now that I saw you
I don't wanna think of anything else now that I thought of you
I've been sleepin' so long in a twenty-year dark night
And now I see daylight, I only see daylight

Luck of the draw only draws the unlucky
And so I became the butt of the joke
I wounded the good and I trusted the wicked
Clearin' the air, I breathed in the smoke

Maybe you ran with the wolves and refused to settle down
Maybe I've stormed out of every single room in this town
Threw out our cloaks and our daggers because it's morning now
It's brighter now, now

I don't wanna look at anything else now that I saw you
(I can never look away)
I don't wanna think of anything else now that I thought of you
(Things will never be the same)
I've been sleepin' so long in a twenty-year dark night
(Now I'm wide awake)
And now I see daylight (Daylight), I only see daylight (Daylight)

I only see daylight, daylight, daylight, daylight
I only see daylight, daylight, daylight, daylight

And I can still see it all (In my mind)
All of you, all of me (Intertwined)
I once believed love would be (Black and white)
But it's golden (Golden)
And I can still see it all (In my head)
Back and forth from New York (Sneakin' in your bed)
I once believed love would be (Burnin' red)
But it's golden
Like daylight, like daylight
Like daylight, daylight

I don't wanna look at anything else now that I saw you
(I can never look away)
And I don't wanna think of anything else now that I thought of you
(Things will never be the same)
I've been sleepin' so long in a twenty-year dark night
(Now I'm wide awake)
And now I see daylight (I see daylight), I only see daylight (Ah)

I only see daylight, daylight, daylight, daylight
I only see daylight, daylight, daylight, daylight (Ah)
(And I can still see it all)
I only see daylight, daylight, daylight, daylight
(And I can still see it all, back and forth from New York)
I only see daylight, daylight, daylight, daylight
(I once believed love would be burning red)

Like daylight
It's golden like daylight
You gotta step into the daylight and let it go
Just let it go, let it go

I wanna be defined by the things that I love
Not the things I hate
Not the things I'm afraid of, I'm afraid of
Or the things that haunt me in the middle of the night
I, I just think that
You are what you love

vocab.definition: Not forgiven for a mistake or wrongdoing. Someone who is unforgiven is still being blamed or punished for something they did.
vocab.lyric_snippet: There are so many lines that I've crossed unforgiven
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express regret and remorse for past actions, acknowledging the harm caused and the need for forgiveness.

vocab.definition: Hurt or damaged, either physically or emotionally. To wound someone means to cause them harm or pain.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I wounded the good and I trusted the wicked
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the harm she caused to good people and her misplaced trust in those with bad intentions.

vocab.definition: A type of sharp, pointed knife used as a weapon. Figuratively, daggers can also refer to sharp, hurtful words or actions.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Threw out our cloaks and our daggers
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to symbolize the end of conflict and the removal of hurtful behaviors in a relationship.

vocab.definition: Connected or linked together, often in a complex way. Intertwined things are closely related or inseparable.
vocab.lyric_snippet: All of you, all of me (Intertwined)
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the deep connection and unity she feels with someone, emphasizing their inseparability.
